title: Tanacetum eginense

intro: Tanacetum eginense, also known as Egin's tansy or Egin's pyrethrum, is a flowering plant native to Central Asia, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, menstrual cramps, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum eginense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in the form of capsules or tablets, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
